responses.prompt: What is the name of your mod?
Which loader or platform best describes the main release target?
Which Minecraft versions are you actively supporting or targeting?
Which categories best describe your mod?
What stage is the mod currently in?
How is the mod currently staffed?
How good do you feel about the mod in its current state?
How likely are you to recommend this mod to players who enjoy its niche?
How confident are you in your next release or update?
Rate the current health of each area of the project.
What is creating the most friction for the mod right now?
Which part of the mod feels strongest right now, and why?
How well is the current modding workflow serving you?
How often are you currently able to work on or ship meaningful progress?
Where are you on the energy-to-burnout spectrum with this mod?
Rank the improvements that would most improve your modding experience.
What part of modding this project still feels harder than it should?
Where do you most often receive player feedback?
How clearly can you tell what players actually want next?
About how many actionable bug reports, suggestions, or issues do you get in a typical week?
Rate how well different player groups are currently served by the mod.
Rank the kinds of player requests you feel most pressure to act on.
What player reaction, story, or use case made you feel the mod was worth building?
Which direction best describes the next phase of the mod?
Which areas are most likely to define your next season of work?
How confident are you that the mod has a healthy future?
How strongly do you agree with the following statements about the mod's future?
What is the single biggest thing that could stop this mod from reaching the future you want for it?
If this mod goes well over the next year, what would success look like to you?

subtitle: A survey for Minecraft mod developers about the state of their mod, the modding experience, and where the project goes next.
title: Minecraft Modder Pulse
